  "weights": {

  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",

    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I focus primarily on A) sentence-/paragraph-level writing polish (R1.6), or B) assessing and tightening the argument—logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment, with light reframing if needed (R1.2, R1.3, R1.1/R1.5)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The paragraph advances a chain from prior work through methods and results to a contribution claim, so checking internal logical consistency is central.",
    "R1.3": "It makes quantitative claims and aligns them with prior findings, so ensuring the claims match the reported analyses and numbers is central (excluding design quality).",
    "R1.6": "Given the brief request to 'take a look' and the single-paragraph artifact, sentence-level clarity, grammar, and flow are likely core expectations.",
    "RX.1": "The request is underspecified, so assumptions and uncertainties about focus should be made explicit.",
    "RX.2": "Any guidance should avoid endorsing stronger claims than what the paragraph supports.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain confined to the provided paragraph without importing external content or unverifiable inferences.",
    "RX.4": "Care is needed to avoid suggestions that could encourage overstatement or misrepresentation of research findings.",
    "RX.5": "Observations should be tied to specific statements in the paragraph to maintain provenance and traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on A) sentence-/paragraph-level writing polish (R1.6), or B) assessing and tightening the argument—logical consistency and evidence–claim alignment, with light reframing if needed (R1.2, R1.3, R1.1/R1.5)?

rationale.R1.2: The paragraph advances a chain from prior work through methods and results to a contribution claim, so checking internal logical consistency is central.
rationale.R1.3: It makes quantitative claims and aligns them with prior findings, so ensuring the claims match the reported analyses and numbers is central (excluding design quality).
rationale.R1.6: Given the brief request to 'take a look' and the single-paragraph artifact, sentence-level clarity, grammar, and flow are likely core expectations.
rationale.RX.1: The request is underspecified, so assumptions and uncertainties about focus should be made explicit.
rationale.RX.2: Any guidance should avoid endorsing stronger claims than what the paragraph supports.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain confined to the provided paragraph without importing external content or unverifiable inferences.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to avoid suggestions that could encourage overstatement or misrepresentation of research findings.
rationale.RX.5: Observations should be tied to specific statements in the paragraph to maintain provenance and traceability.
